title: Multiple Effects and Separation of Concerns
description: Use multiple effects for unrelated logic.

slides.title: One Effect, One Responsibility 🧩
Example
slides.content: Separating a subscription from a data fetch:

quizData.question: It's a good practice to put all side effects into a single useEffect to keep the code concise.
quizData.explanation: No, separating unrelated side effects improves readability and maintainability.
